title: Frank Springer
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
[[File:Frank_springer.jpg|200px|right]]
'''Frank Springer''' ([[December 6]], [[1920s|1929]] – [[April 2]], [[2009]]<ref name="Lambiek">{{WebCite|url=https://www.lambiek.net/artists/s/springer_frank.htm|text=Frank Springer|work=[https://www.lambiek.net Lambiek]|archivedate=20201126045439}}</ref>) was a comic artist who worked on the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic series. In [[1950s|1955]] Springer began in the comics industry as an assistant to [[Wikipedia:George Wunder|George Wunder]] on [[Wikipedia:Terry and the Pirates (comic strip)|''Terry and the Pirates'']]. During the 1960s he worked mainly for [[Wikipedia:Dell Comics|Dell Publishing]] on titles such as [[Wikipedia:Charlie Chan|''Charlie Chan'']] and [[Wikipedia:Brain Boy|''Brain Boy'']]. The following decades through the 1980s Springer worked for a number of publishers including [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]] and [[Marvel Comics]] on titles such as [[Wikipedia:Spider-Man|''Spider-Man'']] and [[Wikipedia:Transformers (comic)|''Transformers'']].

Springer died of prostate cancer on April 2, 2009.<ref name="Newsday">{{WebCite|url=https://www.newsday.com/long-island/nassau/li-comics-artist-frank-springer-dead-at-79-1.1217558|text=LI comics artist Frank Springer dead at 79|author=Barrios, Jennifer|date=2009-04-05|work=[[Wikipedia:Newsday|Newsday]]|archivedate=20201124101844}}</ref>
